title: ficus cocculifolia subsp. sakalavarum
intro: Ficus cocculifolia subsp. sakalavarum, also known as Sakalavaron, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to Madagascar, characterized by its unique foliage and medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ficus cocculifolia subsp. sakalavarum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant's bark and leaves are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes, which are often prepared by boiling the plant parts in water and then straining them.
